text.title: ai-accessibility-automation-2026 Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ai-accessibility-automation-2026. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Treat AI automation as covering 60–70% of detectable WCAG issues; design human review gates for the remaining 30–40%.
rule.rationale: AI tools like axe-core reduce false positives and extract code context, but cannot detect ARIA live-region failures, keyboard traps, focus management issues, or dynamic AT interactions. 94.8% of homepages still fail WCAG 2 checks despite tooling availability — process and accountability drive compliance, not automation alone.

rule.statement: Run AI-powered automated scan at PR level (block on critical/serious violations) and full batch scan weekly (report all levels).
rule.rationale: Different cadences serve different audiences: PR-level catches regressions before merge; weekly gives baseline trend data per WCAG criterion.

rule.statement: Separate issue detection (automated) from issue resolution (human). Never merge these into a single AI step.
rule.rationale: AI can surface and contextualize issues reliably; marking issues as resolved requires human + AT testing validation.

rule.statement: Submit video to captioning service early in the content pipeline, not as a post-publish step.
rule.rationale: Caption APIs return files asynchronously; agents must implement polling with timeout and retry. AI captions achieve ~95% accuracy on standard English speech but degrade significantly with accents, technical terminology, and non-English content — editorial review is required.

rule.statement: Use AI captions as a starting point; require human editorial review before publishing, especially for non-standard speech.
rule.rationale: ADA Title II compliance requires accurate captions; AI accuracy gaps with accented speech and domain terminology create legal exposure without human review.

rule.statement: Use AI-generated VPAT 2.5 drafts as a starting point for annual compliance review only. Human legal review of each conformance claim is mandatory before publishing.
rule.rationale: VPAT criterion language is legally precise; LLM paraphrasing of WCAG success criteria introduces compliance risk. AI-generated VPATs contain factual errors for complex criteria (e.g., 4.1.3 Status Messages in SPAs).

rule.statement: Use exact W3C wording for WCAG success criteria in VPAT claims; do not allow LLM paraphrasing.
rule.rationale: Legal precision is required; paraphrased claims can be challenged and undermine conformance standing.

rule.statement: Integrate axe-core results into the issue tracker automatically; issues not tracked do not get fixed.
rule.rationale: Accessibility issues discovered in CI but not tracked in Jira/Linear are systematically ignored; automated issue creation closes this gap.

rule.statement: Track violation counts per WCAG criterion over time. A rising trend in one criterion indicates a systemic code pattern problem.
rule.rationale: Per-criterion trending surfaces root causes (e.g., new component template missing alt text) faster than per-page reporting.

rule.statement: Pin tool versions (axe-core, pa11y-ci) in CI. AI false-positive reduction behavior changes on tool upgrades.
rule.rationale: AI models in tools like axe DevTools are version-specific; upgrades can change which issues surface, breaking baseline comparisons.

rule.statement: Custom caption-pipeline agent submits video to captioning API, polls for completion, validates output format, and delivers to content team — human reviews quality.
rule.rationale: Submission and polling are mechanical and repeatable; quality review requires human judgment about accuracy for accented speech and domain terminology.
